name: Andhra Pradesh
summary: Andhra Pradesh runs retail through the state-owned APSBCL and has pursued a phased prohibition-leaning policy with high effective prices.
whyCheap: State-controlled retail plus high taxes and policy aimed at curbing consumption keep prices high.

traditional.name: Toddy (Kallu)
traditional.note: A mildly alcoholic fresh palm sap, tapped and drunk locally; a long-standing rural tradition.

name: Arunachal Pradesh
summary: Arunachal Pradesh has relatively liberal liquor availability with lower duties than most large states.
whyCheap: Lower excise duties and local production keep prices modest.

traditional.name: Apong (Poka / Nogin)
traditional.communities: Adi, Galo, Mishing and other communities
traditional.note: A staple rice beer central to social and ritual life, brewed in home and community settings.

name: Assam
summary: Assam uses a private retail model with duty slabs across IMFL, beer and country liquor.
whyCheap: Slab-based excise duty sets the price band.

traditional.name: Apong (Saj / Xaj)
traditional.note: A traditional rice beer of the Mishing people, often served at festivals.

traditional.name: Sujen
traditional.note: A community rice brew with ritual significance.

name: Bihar
summary: Bihar has enforced prohibition since 2016 — no legal retail sale of alcohol.
whyCheap: Prohibition — legal retail sale is banned.

name: Chhattisgarh
summary: Chhattisgarh sells liquor through the state-run CSMCL corporation.
whyCheap: State-run retail model with government-set margins and duty.

traditional.name: Mahua
traditional.note: Distilled from mahua (Madhuca) flowers; culturally central across the tribal belt.

traditional.name: Salfi (Sulfi)
traditional.note: The fermented sap of the sago/salfi palm, known as the 'tree of Bastar'.

name: Goa
summary: Goa is famous for some of the lowest liquor prices in India, driven by comparatively low state excise duty.
whyCheap: Low excise duty and tourism-driven policy keep retail prices well below most states.

traditional.name: Feni
traditional.note: A GI-tagged Goan spirit, made from cashew apple or coconut sap; a heritage product.

name: Gujarat
summary: Gujarat is a dry state; alcohol sale is prohibited except under limited permits.
whyCheap: Prohibition — legal retail sale is not permitted for the general public.

name: Haryana
summary: Haryana is often cheaper than neighbouring Delhi and Chandigarh, drawing NCR buyers.
whyCheap: Comparatively lower duties than parts of NCR.

name: Himachal Pradesh
summary: Himachal Pradesh keeps duties relatively low and is a popular cross-border buy for tourists.
whyCheap: Lower excise duty relative to neighbouring states.

traditional.name: Lugdi / Sur
traditional.note: A traditional home-brew of the Himalayan valleys.

traditional.name: Chhaang
traditional.note: A warming fermented brew common in high-altitude areas.

name: Jharkhand
summary: Jharkhand has shifted between state-run and private retail models with slab-based duty.
whyCheap: Duty slabs and changing retail policy shape prices.

traditional.name: Handia (Hadia)
traditional.communities: Santhal, Munda, Ho and other Adivasi communities
traditional.note: A fermented rice drink integral to festivals, farming and community life.

traditional.name: Mahua
traditional.note: Distilled from mahua flowers; deep cultural and economic roots.

name: Karnataka
summary: Karnataka levies among the highest additional excise duties in India, making the same bottle far costlier than in neighbouring Goa.
whyCheap: High Additional Excise Duty (AED) slabs raise retail prices significantly.

name: Kerala
summary: Kerala routes most retail through the state-run BEVCO, with high effective taxes.
whyCheap: State-controlled retail and high taxes raise prices.

traditional.name: Toddy (Kallu)
traditional.note: A fresh palm sap central to Kerala's toddy-shop culture and cuisine.

name: Madhya Pradesh
summary: Madhya Pradesh uses private retail with periodic policy revisions to its excise structure.
whyCheap: Slab-based excise duty and licensing set prices.

traditional.name: Mahua
traditional.note: Distilled from mahua flowers; MP has moved to promote heritage mahua as a legitimate product.

traditional.name: Handia
traditional.note: A fermented rice brew tied to farming and festivals.

name: Maharashtra
summary: Maharashtra uses multiple duty slabs across IMFL, country liquor and imported spirits.
whyCheap: Layered special/excise duties push prices above low-tax states.

traditional.name: Mahua
traditional.note: A traditional spirit of Maharashtra's tribal belts.

name: Manipur
summary: Manipur enforced prohibition for years and has moved toward partial, regulated relaxation.
whyCheap: Long-standing prohibition with limited, regulated exceptions.

traditional.name: Yu (Atingba / Ashaba)
traditional.note: Atingba is the fermented rice base; Yu the distilled liquor — part of local ritual and cuisine.

name: Meghalaya
summary: Meghalaya has relatively liberal availability with modest duties.
whyCheap: Lower duties than large mainland states.

traditional.name: Kyat (Kiad)
traditional.note: A traditional rice brew and distilled spirit used in social and ritual settings.

name: Mizoram
summary: Mizoram re-imposed prohibition in 2019 after a brief period of regulated sale.
whyCheap: Prohibition — legal retail sale is banned.

traditional.name: Zu
traditional.note: A traditional rice brew of the Mizo hills.

traditional.name: Zawlaidi
traditional.note: A locally made grape wine from Mizoram's wine-growing belt.

name: Nagaland
summary: Nagaland is a dry state under the NLTP Act, though enforcement is debated.
whyCheap: Prohibition — legal retail sale is banned.

traditional.name: Zutho / Thutse
traditional.note: A traditional rice beer brewed for festivals and gatherings across Naga communities.

name: Odisha
summary: Odisha uses a private retail model with slab-based excise duty.
whyCheap: Excise duty slabs set the price band.

traditional.name: Handia
traditional.note: A fermented rice drink woven into farming and festival life.

traditional.name: Mahua
traditional.note: Distilled from mahua flowers; a staple of tribal Odisha.

traditional.name: Salap
traditional.note: The sap of the salap (sago) palm, drunk fresh.

name: Punjab
summary: Punjab's excise policy is a major revenue lever and shifts with each year's excise policy.
whyCheap: Annual excise policy and licence fees drive prices.

name: Rajasthan
summary: Rajasthan applies excise duty plus label and licence fees on liquor.
whyCheap: Duty plus label fees set the price.

traditional.name: Heritage liquors (e.g. Kesar Kasturi, Chandrahas)
traditional.note: Aromatic spirits made from old Rajput recipes, now produced under Rajasthan's regulated heritage-liquor (ASA) framework.

traditional.name: Mahua
traditional.note: Distilled from mahua flowers in the state's tribal areas.

name: Sikkim
summary: Sikkim has notably low liquor prices, aided by local distilling and lower duties.
whyCheap: Local production and lower excise duty keep prices among India's lowest.

traditional.name: Tongba / Chhaang
traditional.note: Millet fermented and drunk warm through a bamboo vessel (tongba) with a straw — an iconic Himalayan ritual.

traditional.name: Raksi
traditional.note: A distilled spirit made from millet or rice.

name: Tamil Nadu
summary: Retail sale runs through the state-owned TASMAC monopoly, a major revenue source for the state.
whyCheap: State monopoly pricing plus duties; revenue-driven.

traditional.name: Toddy (Kallu)
traditional.note: A fresh palm sap with a long rural tradition, though its legal status has shifted over time.

name: Telangana
summary: Telangana has high taxes and a state revenue base heavily reliant on liquor.
whyCheap: High duties and revenue dependence keep prices elevated.

traditional.name: Toddy (Kallu)
traditional.note: A traditional palm sap central to a regulated rural toddy economy.

name: Tripura
summary: Tripura uses a private retail model with standard duty slabs.
whyCheap: Slab-based excise duty sets the price.

traditional.name: Chuwarak
traditional.note: A distilled drink made from rice or fruit, part of Tripuri tradition.

name: Uttar Pradesh
summary: Uttar Pradesh is one of India's largest liquor markets by volume, with slab-based duty.
whyCheap: Large-volume market with slab-based excise duty.

name: Uttarakhand
summary: Uttarakhand uses private retail with duty that shifts across its annual excise policy.
whyCheap: Annual excise policy sets duty and prices.

traditional.name: Kachi / Ghanti
traditional.note: Traditional home-distilled spirits of the Uttarakhand hills.

traditional.name: Chhaang
traditional.note: A warming fermented brew of the upper Himalayas.

name: West Bengal
summary: West Bengal applies an ad valorem duty structure on liquor.
whyCheap: Ad valorem duty ties tax to value, shaping prices.

traditional.name: Handia (Haria)
traditional.communities: Adivasi communities of the western districts
traditional.note: A fermented rice drink common in the state's tribal belt.

traditional.name: Tongba
traditional.note: The warm millet brew of the Darjeeling hills, shared with neighbouring Himalayan cultures.

name: Andaman & Nicobar Islands
summary: The Andaman & Nicobar administration runs liquor retail with relatively modest prices.
whyCheap: Administration-run retail with modest duties.

name: Chandigarh
summary: Chandigarh is often cheaper than neighbouring Punjab and Haryana, a known cross-border buy.
whyCheap: Comparatively lower duties than adjoining states.

name: Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu
summary: Daman & Diu is famous for very low liquor prices, drawing buyers from dry Gujarat nearby.
whyCheap: Very low duties make it one of India's cheapest places to buy liquor.

name: Delhi
summary: Delhi's liquor pricing has swung with repeated excise-policy changes, most notably the 2021-22 excise policy episode, after which retail reverted to government corporations.
whyCheap: Policy volatility has repeatedly reshaped pricing and retail structure.

name: Jammu & Kashmir
summary: Jammu & Kashmir permits regulated liquor retail with slab-based duty.
whyCheap: Slab-based excise duty sets the price.

name: Ladakh
summary: Ladakh permits regulated liquor retail, with limited outlets across the UT.
whyCheap: Limited outlets and duty shape availability and price.

traditional.name: Chhaang
traditional.note: A barley brew traditional to Ladakh's high-altitude culture and festivals.

name: Lakshadweep
summary: Lakshadweep is a dry UT; alcohol sale is prohibited (with limited exceptions historically tied to tourism).
whyCheap: Prohibition — legal retail sale is banned.

name: Puducherry
summary: Puducherry is well known for low liquor prices, a major draw from neighbouring Tamil Nadu.
whyCheap: Low duties make it a famously cheap place to buy liquor.
